idioms.expression: Put my money where my mouth is
idioms.ipa: /pʊt maɪ ˈmʌni wɛər maɪ maʊθ ɪz/
idioms.meaning_en: To prove your words by taking real action.
idioms.meaning_es: Respaldar tus palabras con acciones reales.
idioms.origin_en: American English, 20th century. Comes from gambling and betting — if you believed something strongly, you literally placed money to prove it.
idioms.origin_es: Inglés estadounidense del siglo XX, del mundo de apuestas: si realmente creías en algo, apostabas dinero.
examples: If you believe in the project, put your money where your mouth is.
He finally put his money where his mouth is and invested.

idioms.meaning_en: To stay firm in your decision even under pressure.
idioms.meaning_es: Mantenerse firme en tu decisión aunque haya presión.
idioms.origin_en: Comes from military warfare: soldiers had to stay at their cannons during attacks.
idioms.origin_es: Viene del lenguaje militar: los soldados debían permanecer en sus cañones sin rendirse.
examples: Even if they disagree, stick to your guns.
She stuck to her guns during the meeting.

idioms.meaning_en: A competitor expected to lose, but who fights with determination.
idioms.meaning_es: Competidor que se espera que pierda, pero lucha con determinación.
idioms.origin_en: Comes from 19th-century dogfighting. The dog underneath was considered weaker — the 'underdog'.
idioms.origin_es: Proviene de peleas de perros del siglo XIX. El perro de abajo era visto como el débil: el 'underdog'.
examples: People love cheering for the underdog.
Ecuador is the underdog, but they play with heart.

idioms.meaning_en: Used to encourage someone to continue speaking or acting.
idioms.meaning_es: Usado para animar a alguien a continuar hablando o actuando.
idioms.origin_en: General phrasal verb from Old English 'gān' (to go).
idioms.origin_es: Verbo compuesto del inglés antiguo 'gān' (ir).
examples: Go on, tell me the rest.
Go on! You can do it.

idioms.meaning_en: To leave or disappear.
idioms.meaning_es: Irse o desaparecer.
idioms.origin_en: Literal Old English concept — to move away.
idioms.origin_es: Significado literal: alejarse.
examples: Go away, I’m tired.
The pain will go away soon.

idioms.meaning_en: To record something on paper or a device.
idioms.meaning_es: Anotar algo en papel o dispositivo.
idioms.origin_en: From the verb 'write' + particle 'down' meaning to capture and store information.
idioms.origin_es: Del verbo 'write' + 'down', usado para capturar información.
examples: Write it down so you don’t forget.
I wrote down the instructions.

idioms.meaning_en: Handle whatever remains to be done.
idioms.meaning_es: Encargarse de lo que falta por hacer.
idioms.origin_en: General English construction using the idiomatic verb 'take care of'.
idioms.origin_es: Construcción idiomática con 'take care of' (encargarse de).
examples: Finish the documents, I’ll take care of the rest.
You cook, and I’ll take care of the rest.

idioms.meaning_en: In slang, 'bad' means excellent or impressive.
idioms.meaning_es: En slang, 'bad' significa excelente o impresionante.
idioms.origin_en: African American Vernacular English (AAVE), 1970s music culture.
idioms.origin_es: Del AAVE, cultura musical de los 70.
examples: That car is bad! 😎🔥

idioms.meaning_en: Informal contraction for am not / is not / are not.
idioms.meaning_es: Contracción informal de am not / is not / are not.
idioms.origin_en: Originated as 'an't' in the 1700s.
idioms.origin_es: Originó como 'an't' en los 1700s.
examples: He ain't ready.
This ain't right.
idioms.note: Non-standard English; avoid in formal situations.

idioms.meaning_en: It's not surprising; it makes sense.
idioms.meaning_es: No es de extrañar.
idioms.origin_en: From combining 'no' + 'wonder' (surprise), indicating lack of surprise.
idioms.origin_es: De combinar 'no' + 'wonder' (sorpresa), indicando falta de sorpresa.
examples: No wonder you're tired—you worked all night!

idioms.meaning_en: A question suggesting uncertainty or a possible explanation.
idioms.meaning_es: ¿Podría ser el viento?
idioms.origin_en: Literal question using modal 'could' for speculation.
idioms.origin_es: Pregunta literal usando modal 'could' para especulación.
examples: I heard a strange noise. Could it be the wind?

idioms.meaning_en: The act of finishing or concluding something.
idioms.meaning_es: Finalizando, concluyendo
idioms.origin_en: From wrapping a package, metaphorically completing a task.
idioms.origin_es: De envolver un paquete, metafóricamente completar una tarea.
examples: Let's start wrapping up the meeting.
We are wrapping up the project this week.
Before wrapping up, does anyone have questions?

idioms.meaning_en: A thorough and detailed examination or analysis of something.
idioms.meaning_es: Análisis profundo, mirada detallada
idioms.origin_en: From 'in depth' (thoroughly) + 'look' (examination).
idioms.origin_es: De 'in depth' (profundamente) + 'look' (examen).
examples: The article provides an in-depth look at climate change.
Let's take an in-depth look at the company's finances.
The documentary gives an in-depth look into ancient civilizations.

idioms.meaning_en: A phrase used to indicate maximizing something or getting the greatest benefit.
idioms.meaning_es: Sacar el máximo provecho, aprovechar al máximo
idioms.origin_en: From 'most' (maximum) + 'out' (extracted).
idioms.origin_es: De 'most' (máximo) + 'out' (extraído).
examples: She tries to get the most out of her workday.
Make sure you get the most out of your vacation.
He got the most out of the training session.

idioms.meaning_en: Sharp or acute attention to detail.
idioms.meaning_es: Observación aguda
idioms.origin_en: From 'keen' (sharp) + 'observation' (watching carefully).
idioms.origin_es: De 'keen' (agudo) + 'observation' (observación cuidadosa).
examples: Her keen observation helped solve the case.

idioms.meaning_en: Refers to something lacking originality or creativity.
idioms.meaning_es: Molde de galletas, genérico, uniforme
idioms.origin_en: From a tool used to cut cookies into identical shapes, suggesting uniformity.
idioms.origin_es: De una herramienta usada para cortar galletas en formas idénticas, sugiriendo uniformidad.
examples: The neighborhood is full of cookie-cutter houses.
This story feels like a cookie-cutter romance.
Use the cookie cutter to make star-shaped cookies.

idioms.meaning_en: To participate, attract, or involve someone in something.
idioms.meaning_es: Participar, involucrar, atraer
idioms.origin_en: From Old French 'engagier', meaning 'to pledge'.
idioms.origin_es: Del francés antiguo 'engagier', que significa 'comprometer'.
examples: She engages with her community by volunteering.
The teacher engaged the students with interactive activities.
This book really engages the reader.

idioms.meaning_en: Small parts of something.
idioms.meaning_es: Pedazos, partes
idioms.origin_en: From 'bits' (small pieces) + 'pieces' (fragments), emphasizing smallness.
idioms.origin_es: De 'bits' (pedazos pequeños) + 'pieces' (fragmentos), enfatizando pequeñez.
examples: I gathered the bits and pieces of the puzzle.

idioms.meaning_en: You are crazy or irrational.
idioms.meaning_es: Estás loco
idioms.origin_en: From slang use of 'nuts' meaning crazy, possibly from 'off one's nut' (head).
idioms.origin_es: Del uso del slang 'nuts' significando loco, posiblemente de 'off one's nut' (cabeza).
examples: You are nuts if you think that will work!

idioms.meaning_en: Used to emphasize a negative statement or question; in any way or to any degree
idioms.meaning_es: En absoluto, para nada; de ninguna manera
idioms.origin_en: From Middle English 'at alle', meaning 'in any way' or 'wholly'.
idioms.origin_es: Del inglés medio 'at alle', que significa 'de ninguna manera' o 'completamente'.
examples: I don't like it at all.
Do you speak French at all?
She's not tired at all.
Is there any hope at all?
I can't see anything at all.
Not at all! (You're welcome / It's no problem)

idioms.meaning_en: Being in a group makes you safer or more protected than being alone
idioms.meaning_es: Estar en grupo te hace más seguro o protegido que estar solo
idioms.origin_en: Ancient concept dating back to survival instincts. Animals and humans historically traveled in groups for protection from predators and threats. The phrase became popular in English in the 19th century.
idioms.origin_es: Concepto antiguo que se remonta a los instintos de supervivencia. Animales y humanos históricamente viajaban en grupos para protegerse de depredadores y amenazas. La frase se popularizó en inglés en el siglo XIX.
examples: Let's all walk home together—there's safety in numbers.
The protesters believe there's safety in numbers.
In the wild, many animals travel in herds because of safety in numbers.
Don't go alone at night; there's safety in numbers.

idioms.expression: Work one's butt off / Bust one's butt off
idioms.ipa: /wɜːrk wʌnz bʌt ɔːf/ | /bʌst wʌnz bʌt ɔːf/
idioms.meaning_en: To work extremely hard; to put in maximum effort and dedication; to work tirelessly and intensely (informal/slang)
idioms.meaning_es: Trabajar extremadamente duro; poner el máximo esfuerzo y dedicación; trabajar incansablemente e intensamente (informal/jerga)
idioms.origin_en: American slang from the mid-20th century. 'Butt' is informal for buttocks/rear end. The hyperbolic expression 'off' suggests working so hard that you'd lose part of your body. Similar expressions include 'work one's tail off' or 'work one's ass off' (more vulgar). The idiom emphasizes extreme physical or mental exertion.
idioms.origin_es: Jerga estadounidense de mediados del siglo XX. 'Butt' es informal para nalgas/trasero. La expresión hiperbólica 'off' sugiere trabajar tan duro que perderías parte de tu cuerpo. Expresiones similares incluyen 'work one's tail off' o 'work one's ass off' (más vulgar). El modismo enfatiza esfuerzo físico o mental extremo.

simple_present: She works her butt off every single day to support her family. 💪👨‍👩‍👧
My brother busts his butt off at two jobs to pay for college. 🎓💼
Athletes work their butts off during training season preparing for competitions. 🏋️⚽
present_continuous: I'm working my butt off to finish this project before the deadline. ⏰💻
The entire team is busting their butts off to meet the impossible quota. 👥😰
She's working her butt off studying for the medical board exams. 📚🩺
simple_past: I worked my butt off all semester and finally got straight A's. 📖✅
They busted their butts off renovating the entire house in three months. 🏠🔨
My parents worked their butts off to give us a better life. 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦❤️
past_continuous: While everyone else was partying, I was working my butt off on my thesis. 🎉📝
The construction crew was busting their butts off in the scorching heat. ☀️😓
She was working her butt off when the promotion opportunity finally came. 💼📈
present_perfect: I have worked my butt off for this company for ten years straight. 🏢⏰
The team has busted their butts off and deserves recognition for their efforts. 🏆👏
You've worked your butt off; you deserve a vacation now. ✈️🏖️
present_perfect_continuous: I have been working my butt off on this startup for three years. 🚀💼
She has been busting her butt off training for the Olympics since childhood. 🏅👧
We have been working our butts off trying to save this failing business. 📊💪
past_perfect: By graduation, I had worked my butt off and achieved my dream GPA. 🎓✨
They had busted their butts off before the investors finally took notice. 💰👀
She had worked her butt off long before anyone recognized her talent. 🎭⭐
past_perfect_continuous: I had been working my butt off for months before getting that promotion. 📈💼
The team had been busting their butts off for years before winning the championship. 🏆⚽
She had been working her butt off silently while others took the credit. 😔💪
simple_future: I will work my butt off to prove I deserve this opportunity. 💪🎯
The team will bust their butts off to win the national tournament. 🏀🏆
She will work her butt off until she achieves her career goals. 📊✨
future_continuous: This time next month, I'll be working my butt off preparing for finals. 📚😰
The athletes will be busting their butts off during summer training camp. ☀️🏋️
By next week, everyone will be working their butts off before the audit. 📋👔
future_perfect: By December, I will have worked my butt off for this degree for four years. 🎓📅
By the tournament, they will have busted their butts off training intensely. 🏆💪
By retirement, she will have worked her butt off for forty years straight. 👵⏰
future_perfect_continuous: By next year, I will have been working my butt off on this project for five years. 📅💼
By Olympics, the athletes will have been busting their butts off for a decade. 🏅⏰
By 2030, she will have been working her butt off as a surgeon for twenty years. 🩺📅
modal_perfect_should: You should've worked your butt off instead of procrastinating all semester. 📚😔
They should've busted their butts off earlier to avoid this crisis. ⚠️⏰
I should've worked my butt off when I had the chance. 💼😞
modal_perfect_could: I could've worked my butt off and achieved more if I'd been more focused. 🎯💭
We could've busted our butts off and finished ahead of schedule. ⏰✅
She could've worked her butt off but chose work-life balance instead. ⚖️😌
modal_perfect_would: I would've worked my butt off but health issues prevented me. 🏥😔
They would've busted their butts off if the project hadn't been cancelled. 📋❌
She would've worked her butt off for that promotion opportunity gladly. 💼✨
modal_perfect_might: He might've worked his butt off but still didn't get the recognition. 💪😞
The team might've busted their butts off and still lost the competition. 🏆💔
I might've worked my butt off more if I'd known the stakes. 🎯❓

additional_contexts: My mom worked her butt off raising five kids alone. 👩‍👧‍👦❤️
Stop making excuses and bust your butt off if you want results! 💪🔥
I've been working my butt off and I'm exhausted. 😰💼
She busted her butt off and became the youngest CEO in company history. 👩‍💼🌟
They work their butts off every day but barely make ends meet. 💰😔

idioms.meaning_en: To stay awake all night, especially to study or work; to work or study through the entire night without sleeping
idioms.meaning_es: Quedarse despierto toda la noche, especialmente para estudiar o trabajar; trabajar o estudiar durante toda la noche sin dormir
idioms.origin_en: American college slang from the 1960s. 'All-nighter' literally refers to something that lasts 'all night'. The verb 'pull' here means 'to carry out' or 'accomplish'. Originally associated with cramming for exams, it expanded to include any overnight work session. The term became widespread on university campuses and later entered mainstream vocabulary.
idioms.origin_es: Jerga universitaria estadounidense de la década de 1960. 'All-nighter' literalmente se refiere a algo que dura 'toda la noche'. El verbo 'pull' aquí significa 'llevar a cabo' o 'lograr'. Originalmente asociado con estudiar intensivamente para exámenes, se expandió para incluir cualquier sesión de trabajo nocturna. El término se generalizó en campus universitarios y luego entró al vocabulario general.

simple_present: College students often pull all-nighters during final exam week every semester. 📚😴
My roommate pulls an all-nighter at least twice a month to meet deadlines. ⏰💻
Doctors in residency frequently pull all-nighters at the hospital on call. 🏥👨‍⚕️
present_continuous: I'm pulling an all-nighter tonight to finish my thesis before submission. 📝☕
The development team is pulling an all-nighter to fix the critical bug. 💻🌙
She's pulling an all-nighter studying for the medical board exam tomorrow. 📖🩺
simple_past: I pulled an all-nighter and finally completed the entire project successfully. ✅😴
They pulled an all-nighter before the product launch and it paid off. 🚀💼
My study group pulled an all-nighter cramming for the chemistry final. 🧪📚
past_continuous: While everyone else was sleeping, I was pulling an all-nighter on my essay. 😴📝
The programmers were pulling an all-nighter when the server crashed again. 💻💥
She was pulling an all-nighter preparing for her dissertation defense presentation. 🎓📊
present_perfect: I have pulled so many all-nighters this semester that I'm completely exhausted. 😰📚
The team has pulled three all-nighters this week alone to meet the deadline. ⏰💼
She has never pulled an all-nighter in her entire academic career. 🎓✨
present_perfect_continuous: I have been pulling all-nighters regularly and my health is suffering badly. 😷📉
The startup team has been pulling all-nighters for months before the launch. 🚀⏰
He has been pulling all-nighters trying to save his failing business desperately. 💼😰
past_perfect: By graduation, I had pulled more all-nighters than I could count. 🎓😴
They had pulled an all-nighter before the investors' meeting that morning. 💰☕
She had never pulled an all-nighter until medical school started suddenly. 🏥📚
past_perfect_continuous: I had been pulling all-nighters for weeks before finally collapsing from exhaustion. 😵💫
The animators had been pulling all-nighters for months before the movie premiered. 🎬🌙
He had been pulling all-nighters studying before acing the bar exam. ⚖️📖
simple_future: I will pull an all-nighter tonight to prepare for tomorrow's presentation. 📊☕
The engineering team will pull an all-nighter to launch the app on time. 📱⏰
Students will pull countless all-nighters during finals week as usual. 📚🌙
future_continuous: This time tomorrow night, I'll be pulling an all-nighter finishing my dissertation. 📝😴
The crew will be pulling an all-nighter shooting the final scenes tonight. 🎬🌙
By Friday, developers will be pulling an all-nighter debugging the new software. 💻🐛
future_perfect: By Monday morning, I will have pulled five all-nighters this week straight. 😵⏰
By the deadline, the team will have pulled countless all-nighters together. 💼🌙
By exam day, students will have pulled multiple all-nighters cramming desperately. 📚😰
future_perfect_continuous: By next month, I will have been pulling all-nighters for an entire year. 📅😴
By launch day, the team will have been pulling all-nighters for six months. 🚀⏰
By graduation, she will have been pulling all-nighters throughout her medical training. 🎓🏥
modal_perfect_should: I should've pulled an all-nighter instead of sleeping through my alarm. ⏰😔
You should've pulled an all-nighter to finish instead of procrastinating earlier. 📚💔
We should've pulled an all-nighter preparing for that crucial presentation properly. 📊😞
modal_perfect_could: I could've pulled an all-nighter but decided to prioritize my health. 😴💪
We could've pulled an all-nighter and finished the entire project yesterday. ✅⏰
She could've pulled an all-nighter studying but chose to rest instead. 📖😌
modal_perfect_would: I would've pulled an all-nighter but my doctor warned against it. 🏥⚠️
They would've pulled an all-nighter if the deadline hadn't been extended. 📅✅
She would've pulled an all-nighter but her roommate needed quiet sleep. 🤫😴
modal_perfect_might: I might've pulled an all-nighter but I was already too exhausted. 😴💤
The team might've pulled an all-nighter if management had provided better resources. 💼📉
He might've pulled an all-nighter and still failed the exam anyway. 📚😔

additional_contexts: I need to pull an all-nighter tonight—my term paper is due tomorrow morning! 📝⏰
Pulling all-nighters regularly is terrible for your health and productivity long-term. 😷📉
We pulled an all-nighter binge-watching the entire series together last weekend. 📺🍿
My dad pulled an all-nighter at the office before his big career presentation. 💼👔
After pulling three all-nighters this week, I finally crashed for 14 hours. 😴💤
